title: Best ETFs in Canada (2026): Top Picks by Category
description: Discover the best ETFs in Canada for 2026. Compare XEQT, VFV, XIC and more by category, including MER, historical returns, and AUM to build your portfolio.

mainEntity.name: What is the best ETF in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 'best' ETF depends on your goals. For a globally diversified, all-in-one portfolio, XEQT and VEQT are top choices. For US exposure, VFV is highly popular.

mainEntity.name: Are Canadian ETFs a good investment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, ETFs offer instant diversification, low fees (MERs), and easy access to global and domestic markets, making them excellent long-term investments.

mainEntity.name: What is the best Canadian dividend ETF?

acceptedAnswer.text: VDY is consistently ranked as one of the best Canadian dividend ETFs, offering high yield from top Canadian banks and energy companies.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between XEQT and VEQT?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both are 100% equity all-in-one ETFs. XEQT has slightly higher US exposure and a slightly lower MER, while VEQT holds more Canadian equities. Performance is historically very similar.

mainEntity.name: Which ETF tracks the S&P 500 in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: VFV is the most popular Canadian-listed ETF that tracks the S&P 500 (unhedged), offering direct exposure to the largest US companies.
